mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains in the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2011-11-15 indicates sustained zero-rate policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market tightening and rising consumer income expectations, which drives equity valuations higher through increased earnings growth expectations. The mechanism operates over a 60-day horizon as labor market signals propagate through earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points in the prior month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE declines > 0.2pp are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is positive (opposite of predicted direction) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move together as joint indicators of consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth. The interaction effect is stronger than either series alone because it confirms demand breadth across housing and consumption.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month in the same period
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 399349M and HOUST at 628K as of 2011-10-15
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST growth are not significantly higher than returns following single-series growth (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%, signaling a contraction in real economic activity that precedes equity earnings downgrades. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC is positive (opposite of predicted direction) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) move in opposite directions as joint labor market indicators. When PAYEMS grows month-over-month while UNRATE falls simultaneously, this dual confirmation of labor strength signals sustained consumer income growth and equity earnings support over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month AND UNRATE declines in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131708K and UNRATE at 8.6% as of 2011-11-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns following dual PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy pressure that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth. In a regime of monetary tightening expectations, this mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when CPI YoY > 2.5%, or correlation between CPI changes and forward returns is negative (opposite of predicted direction) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as the broadest demand signal. When GDPC1 growth rate declines quarter-over-quarter by more than 0.3 percentage points, this signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces equity earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate declines by more than 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following GDPC1 growth deceleration > 0.3pp, or Rank IC is positive (opposite of predicted direction) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling a synchronized contraction in real economic capacity and labor utilization. This dual confirmation of supply-side weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward in response to reduced productive capacity.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.8263 and PAYEMS at 131708K as of 2011-11-15
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following dual INDPRO and PAYEMS declines, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05 in rolling regressions.
